description_long: This is a roughly hewn, irregularly shaped stone block with a predominantly bluish-gray coloration and a coarse, uneven texture. The stone features jagged edges and fractured surfaces, with some faces appearing more angular and others more sloped or chipped. There are visible patches of lighter and darker tones, as well as faint markings or numbers on one of the flatter surfaces, possibly for identification or cataloging. The stone's surface is matte and rough, with no signs of polish or artificial treatment, suggesting it is in a natural or minimally processed state. A small white label is affixed to one edge, indicating it may be part of a collection or used for study. The overall appearance is that of a dense, solid rock fragment suitable for use as an obstacle or barrier in an urban or landscaped environment.
description: A jagged, bluish-gray stone block with rough, fractured surfaces and visible markings, appearing dense and unpolished.
description_view_0: The stone's broadest, flattest face is visible, showing a rough, uneven surface with a prominent sloped edge and a small white label.
description_view_1: This view highlights the stone's angular side, with a darker, more shadowed surface and the label partially visible on the upper edge.
description_view_2: The stone's lighter-toned face is shown, featuring visible blue markings and a sharp, irregular edge.
description_view_3: A side view reveals the stone's layered, striated texture and a more compact, block-like profile.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a barrier along a path
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and faint veining, with some natural layering and visible catalog markings.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with minor chipping and surface abrasions indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; label suggests it may be part of a curated collection.
